RaceName: Widmer Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Has taken well to fences at around this distance, winning at Stratford and Fontwell before unseating at the last when still in front at Stratford (2m4f, good to firm) last month; she must come under consideration to get back on track.

RaceName: Widmer Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Nothing special over hurdles but made an immediate impact over fences, scoring at this track (2m, good; 28-1) last month; unable to replicate that form when beaten 47l over the same C&D three weeks ago and needs to bounce back on this step back up in trip.

RaceName: Widmer Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Off the mark on his fifth attempt over fences at Fontwell (2m2f, good) in May and made the winner pull out all the stops when second over C&D (good) last month; in decent nick at present and looks sure to give a good account.

RaceName: Widmer Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: His sole success over fences came at Market Rasen (2m1f, soft) last July off 2lb higher; didn't offer a great deal once headed when third at Southwell (2m5f, good) last month in a first-time visor (retained) but has dropped to a career-low mark with Sean Bowen on board so all is not lost.

RaceName: Widmer Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Two of his four wins over fences have been over C&D but he has been well below that level recently and is hard to fancy.

RaceName: Widmer Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: 0-9 under rules; not a bad effort when runner-up on her chasing debut at Southwell (2m5f, good) last month but beaten 23l over C&D since; needs to show more.

RaceName: Widmer Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Twice placed in 2m4f hurdle races here last summer (good) but ended last year's campaign on a low, achieving little on October's chasing debut; can't be certain he will hit the ground running in 2026.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Restricted Maiden Stakes (For Horses In Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: By Havana Gold; improved on debut run at Wolverhampton when 6.5l third at Kempton last time (7f; 12-1); quite interesting on turf debut.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Restricted Maiden Stakes (For Horses In Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Home-bred; beaten over 17l when 11th of 12 in a Chester novice on debut this month (6f, good; 150-1); should come on for the run.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Restricted Maiden Stakes (For Horses In Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Improved on his debut run (6f, AW) when 3l seventh of 18 in a Newbury novice this month (6f, good to firm; 25-1, Aljoon back in tenth on debut); should improve again at 7f.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Restricted Maiden Stakes (For Horses In Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 9,000euros yearling; well-beaten 28-1 shot on Thirsk debut (6f, good to firm); can do better with that run under his belt.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Restricted Maiden Stakes (For Horses In Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 5,000gns breeze-up buy in April; beaten over 6l when tenth of 18 on Newbury debut 13 days ago (6f, good to firm; 33-1, Macau seventh but had already had a run), keeping on after missing a beat at the start; interesting one.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Restricted Maiden Stakes (For Horses In Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Bought for £18,000 at a breeze-up; filly by Bayside Boy; well beaten on last month's debut at Pontefract (6f, hood; 16-1) but should come on for that; hood now discarded

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Restricted Maiden Stakes (For Horses In Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Home-bred filly by Twilight Son; sister to an Italian 6f winner; dam ran twice at 1m2f; worth a market check on debut

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Restricted Maiden Stakes (For Horses In Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 95,000gns yearling but re-sold for £20,000 at a breeze-up; tenth foal; half-sister to four winners, one over hurdles; dam placed 8.6f AW/1m4f turf; could go well on debut.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Restricted Maiden Stakes (For Horses In Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Kameko filly who shaped quite well when fifth of ten on her debut at Ayr (7f, good; 40-1); should be sharper this time

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Restricted Maiden Stakes (For Horses In Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: By smart sprinter Naval Crown; half-sister to three winners (one over hurdles); dam Group-placed 6f 2yo winner; worth a betting check.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Restricted Maiden Stakes (For Horses In Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: By Arizona; half-sister to four winners; shaped well when fourth on her debut at Newcastle (7f; 25-1); should come on for that experience

RaceName: Wexford Town Mares Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Bumper and hurdle winner ran her best race over fences when second at Tramore in May over this trip; poor back over hurdles at Sligo last time and others have stronger claims.

RaceName: Wexford Town Mares Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Point, bumper (here) and dual hurdle winner began her chasing career with a solid second at Cork last November; has been disappointing twice since including a poor effort back here last time; questions now.

RaceName: Wexford Town Mares Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Smart hurdler won a Grade 3 mares' novice at Down Royal last November; well beaten in the Cheltenham mares' novice after a break and was poor in a Punchestown handicap since; however, point winner ran well over fences on second go last year so is respected.

RaceName: Wexford Town Mares Beginners Chase
Spotlight: One of two runners for yard; point, bumper and hurdle winner is rated 129 in the latter sphere though she didn't really progress; looked like winning over 2m3f here on second chase start but maybe got outstayed; back to this trip should suit and a big player.

RaceName: Wexford Town Mares Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Pulled-up in three of four point starts this year; can't be fancied against these on rules debut.

RaceName: Wexford Town Mares Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Second runner for yard; bumper and dual hurdle winner wasn't at her best on last two 2025 hurdle runs; returned from an absence with a sound chasing debut at Killarney in May; should be better for that and looks a big player.

RaceName: Land O Plums Clives Of Copthorne Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Scored for the second time over fences at Market Rasen (3m, good) in April when making all; pulled up after not being allowed an uncontested lead over C&D (good) the following month; still holds claims if on his game.

RaceName: Land O Plums Clives Of Copthorne Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Signed off with Henry Daly with her second win over fences, at Ludlow (2m4f, good) in May; subsequently sold for £22,000 and made an immediate impact for her new stable when securing wins at Uttoxeter (3m, good) and Market Rasen (2m6f, good); this will be tougher but she's in top form and should go close.

RaceName: Land O Plums Clives Of Copthorne Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: All three chase wins have been over C&D, including in this equivalent race in 2023; equally effective over hurdles with three wins, the most recent at Uttoxeter (3m, good) last month; last of five finishers when back over fences at Cartmel (3m1f, good) last time but cannot be dismissed around here.

RaceName: Land O Plums Clives Of Copthorne Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Bumper and dual hurdle winner; made a promising start over fences when runner-up over C&D (good) in May; may have had his stamina stretched when last of four at Newton Abbot (3m2f, good) last month and may warrant another chance.

RaceName: Land O Plums Clives Of Copthorne Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Dual hurdle winner whose only win over fences (seven starts) came at the expense of two rivals at Newton Abbot in April 2025 (2m5f, good); last three runs, in particular, leave him with some questions to answer right now.

RaceName: Land O Plums Clives Of Copthorne Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Has only finished out of the first two twice in 11 runs over fences; should be sharper for her second at Stratford (2m7f, good) last month, following an eight-month absence, and holds place claims at least.

RaceName: Land O Plums Clives Of Copthorne Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Hasn't won since landing a Warwick maiden hurdle in April 2024; has an inconsistent profile but ran well on his second start over fences when runner-up to Axel Bleue over C&D (good) three weeks ago; that run puts him in with a shout.

RaceName: Land O Plums Clives Of Copthorne Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Landed a couple of bumpers last year and got his act together on his third chase start when scoring over C&D (good) three weeks ago, just ahead of Gwash; there should be more to come with that first win under his belt and he's another with possibilities.

RaceName: Land O Plums Clives Of Copthorne Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Won twice over C&D last summer but has been beaten off this mark three times this term and likely needs to come down the weights before he can be seriously considered.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Five-race maiden who has been placed (over 6f) on last three starts, latest in first-time cheekpieces at Nottingham (nursery debut) when below form but shaping as if he's ready for a crack at 7f; solid claims.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Has shown improvement in a couple of maiden races (AW/turf) since upped to 7f, creditable fourth at Chepstow latest; bred to do better still, being half-brother to two winners for his stable; interesting nursery debutant.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Has failed to land a blow in 6f contests; latest effort (seemed to run well at 250-1 odds) possibly isn't reliable and opening mark may overestimate him; figures aside, he looks the type who has perhaps been crying out for a 7f nursery.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Gelded prior to running well in first-time cheekpieces at Leicester (7f, good to firm) last week on nursery debut, finishing second of ten; may well build on that effort and take another step forward; leading player off same mark.

RaceName: Wexfordraces.ie Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Bumper winner for Nicky Henderson in 2024 but lightly-raced since, latterly for this yard; not a bad chase debut at Punchestown last November but was pulled-up at Kilbeggan 12 days ago so questions to answer.

RaceName: Wexfordraces.ie Beginners Chase
OwnerName: Mrs Suzanne McAuley & Ms C A Jennings
Spotlight: Yet to win on the Flat but ran a fine race in a Killarney maiden over 1m last time; juvenile hurdle winner in 2024 was disappointing when last seen in that sphere; has a bit to find on chasing debut.

RaceName: Wexfordraces.ie Beginners Chase
Spotlight: One of two runners for yard; bumper and maiden hurdle winner for John Queally has had seven chase runs, with best effort a close second at Tipperary last year; has a shout if at his best on return for a new yard.

RaceName: Wexfordraces.ie Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Second runner for yard; dual hurdle winner in 2023 scored on the Flat in 2024 and some good hurdle runs in defeat too; likely best watched on return from a long absence and chasing debut.

RaceName: Wexfordraces.ie Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Smart hurdler has run some fine races in defeat over fences; best effort when second in a 2m1f Cork handicap off a mark of 130 in April; not as good twice since but cheekpieces are added and a big player if at his best.

RaceName: Wexfordraces.ie Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Maiden and novice hurdle winner last season up to 2m6f but lost his form afterwards; rebounded with a fine effort over C&D on chasing debut, running on late; has a shout if building on that.

RaceName: Colin Jones Memorial Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Qualifier) (GBB) (Div I)
Spotlight: Has shown promise over hurdles; caught the attention of the stewards last time; may be more interesting in handicaps but he is one of the better types in this field, being a dual Flat winner.

RaceName: Colin Jones Memorial Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Qualifier) (GBB) (Div I)
Spotlight: Rules debutant who has to overcome a long absence and holds poor claims on his point form (bumpers/fences); best watched unless the market suggests otherwise.

RaceName: Colin Jones Memorial Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Qualifier) (GBB) (Div I)
Spotlight: Took seven attempts to open his account in Irish points; folded tamely over 2m4f switched to rules last time; drops further in distance.

RaceName: Colin Jones Memorial Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Qualifier) (GBB) (Div I)
Spotlight: Low-grade maiden on the Flat; modest fifth over C&D in latest attempt over hurdles; no certainty to improve on that effort.

RaceName: Colin Jones Memorial Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Qualifier) (GBB) (Div I)
Spotlight: Stablemate of Iwanttobreakfree; has cut little ice in three races (both hurdles attempts over C&D) and looks one who'll be more interesting when qualified for handicaps.

RaceName: Colin Jones Memorial Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Qualifier) (GBB) (Div I)
Spotlight: Comfortably held over C&D (hurdles debut) last time but this looks a weaker race; one of the main players on his bumper form, which culminated in third-place finish at Worcester.

RaceName: Colin Jones Memorial Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Qualifier) (GBB) (Div I)
Spotlight: Has shown some promise in Irish point and Uttoxeter bumper, finishing second then third; represents the champion trainer and remains open to progress; respected hurdles debutant.

RaceName: Colin Jones Memorial Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Qualifier) (GBB) (Div I)
Spotlight: Record of 0-5 on Flat and 0-3 over hurdles but he has proved broadly consistent and penultimate effort has worked out well; creditable third over C&D latest; solid claims.

RaceName: Colin Jones Memorial Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Qualifier) (GBB) (Div I)
Spotlight: Thrice-raced mare who isn't solid on form; recorded a meagre RPR over C&D on hurdles/stable/seasonal debut.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap
OwnerName: Ursa Major Racing Ltd And Partner
Spotlight: Last turf win was at Redcar in September 2024 (6f, good to firm; blinkers on for the first time); ran one of his best races this season when fourth over 1m at Ayr last week (1m, good to firm; visor on); same headgear; each-way chance.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap
Spotlight: Three-time C&D winner (twice on soft, once on good to firm); runner-up over C&D on first two starts this season (good); also a good third here two runs back but ran poorly latest, on his first run on good to firm this season; perhaps ideally suited by softer nowadays.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap
Spotlight: Won his first race since September 2024 when scoring at Chester last time (7f, good; third run in cheekpieces, runner-up third there the next day); up 3lb; goes well on fast ground; a possible.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap
Spotlight: Two good runs in June, winning at Wolverhampton (7f) and finishing a close second at Carlisle (7f, good) to a subsequent winner; a possible.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap
Spotlight: Off the mark for the season when making all at Pontefract last time (6f, good); his other five wins have all been over 7f (two sound runs on both C&D starts); well drawn; a possible.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap
Spotlight: Unexposed; his two best runs have been over C&D, latest when fourth on second handicap start (good to firm; did best of the hold-up horses); chance.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap
Spotlight: Ex-John Ryan; yet to hit top form for new yard, but his handicap mark has dropped significantly; could go well with a visor on for first time.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap
Spotlight: C&D winner in June 2024 (good to firm) and 7f Musselburgh winner last August; has not been in the best of form this season, but 4lb lower than last winning mark.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Dual hurdle winner at Clonmel is yet to score over fences but has posted several solid runs in defeat; beaten 7l at Kilbeggan over 2m4f but was out of his comfort zone early over C&D last time; the cheekpieces worn then are removed; needs best.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Chase
OwnerName: Paul M Butler & James Patrick Byrne
Spotlight: Two of his three hurdle wins came at this track; some solid runs in defeat in beginners' chases but seemed to do too much in front when well beaten in a C&D handicap last time; can do better with more restraint.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Three-time hurdle winner scored sole chase win at Down Royal in November 2024; however, lost his form last summer and two runs this year don't inspire confidence.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Second runner for yard; point winner won a maiden hurdle at this track last August but was hit and miss in handicaps after; solid chase debut at Tramore behind a mare who won in Killarney last week; looks a player.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Won a 2m4f maiden hurdle at this track last year; hit and miss since but her best chase run came in a C&D handicap two starts ago; well below that level since and has to rebound.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Third runner for yard; maiden over hurdles but opened her account in a 2m2f handicap chase at Thurles last November; has been below that level thrice since and needs a return to best.

RaceName: Adare Manor Opportunity Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Dual point winner won a Limerick maiden hurdle in 2024; two solid chase runs in 2025 but lost his form since and has to do better; drops in trip.

RaceName: Colin Jones Memorial Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Qualifier) (GBB) (Div II)
Spotlight: Record of just 1-20 on Flat, the success in Class 6 handicap over 1m2f in April; form dipped last time; brings limited potential to this change of code.

RaceName: Colin Jones Memorial Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Qualifier) (GBB) (Div II)
Spotlight: Best effort in bumpers when third at Worcester; soundly beaten over C&D switched to hurdles last time, however, and may be more interesting in handicaps later on.

RaceName: Colin Jones Memorial Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Qualifier) (GBB) (Div II)
Spotlight: Not the force of old (one-time useful Flat handicapper) but has shown consistent hurdles form at Stratford this summer, close second to odds-on shot over 2m last time; solid claims.

RaceName: Colin Jones Memorial Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Qualifier) (GBB) (Div II)
Spotlight: Beaten about 14l in both attempts over hurdles but likely to fare better today as this looks an easier assignment and he's one of the stronger options on Flat form, which includes a win.

RaceName: Colin Jones Memorial Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Qualifier) (GBB) (Div II)
Spotlight: Scored by wide margin, albeit in weak race, at Stratford on final bumper start; finished 12l behind Penzance over hurdles there since but may do better still; one of the main players.

RaceName: Colin Jones Memorial Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Qualifier) (GBB) (Div II)
Spotlight: Represents major stable but was friendless in the betting and well beaten in Warwick contest in March; had wind surgery since; the market may again guide the way.

RaceName: Colin Jones Memorial Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Qualifier) (GBB) (Div II)
Spotlight: Record of 0-6 on Flat and 0-4 over hurdles but has largely consistent form, creditable third over C&D most recently; this race is of a similar standard; again has place claims.

RaceName: Colin Jones Memorial Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Qualifier) (GBB) (Div II)
Spotlight: Has shown poor form on Flat for current yard and remains a maiden under that code; pulled up in both hurdles attempts; enough to prove back in this sphere.

RaceName: Colin Jones Memorial Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Qualifier) (GBB) (Div II)
Spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who was successful (at about 1m4f; fair form) in last two Flat starts for Kevin Philippart de Foy, latest in October; sold for 52,000gns and gelded since; interesting, assuming he transfers ability to hurdles.

RaceName: racingtv.com/freetrial Handicap
Spotlight: Maiden; modest form at up to 1m4f on the Flat; last of four in a juvenile hurdle at Market Rasen on latest start; has since had wind surgery; potential improver.

RaceName: racingtv.com/freetrial Handicap
Spotlight: Unexposed; ran her best race when a staying-on third of six over 1m2f at Nottingham last time (1m2f, good to firm), shaping as though a further step up in trip would suit; contender.

RaceName: racingtv.com/freetrial Handicap
Spotlight: Off the mark on first run at 1m4f (had been tried over 1m6f previously) when winning by a short head at Ripon last time (good; blinkers back on; first two well clear); the second won by 8l at Pontefract on Friday, so this 3yo may well be well in off just 4lb higher; the one to beat.

RaceName: racingtv.com/freetrial Handicap
Spotlight: Met trouble at the start when just over 7l behind Riddikulus at Ripon last time (1m4f, good) but better is needed to reverse the placings with just a 6lb swing.

RaceName: racingtv.com/freetrial Handicap
OwnerName: Clova Farms And P D Niven
Spotlight: Unexposed; has not shown a great deal, beaten just over 6l when eighth of ten at Wetherby on last month's handicap debut (1m2f, good to firm; 33-1); should improve at 1m4f, but plenty to find.

RaceName: racingtv.com/freetrial Handicap
Spotlight: Placed in a couple of Newcastle handicaps on 1m2f in April/May but has not done as well on last two runs, latest when tried at 1m4f (weakened over 1f out); plenty to prove on turf debut.

RaceName: racingtv.com/freetrial Handicap
OwnerName: The Tokyo Bay Syndicate And P Kirby
Spotlight: Unexposed; well beaten at triple-figure odds in three novice/maidens at up to 1m on the AW; may improve significantly up in trip on handicap/turf debut.

RaceName: Support The Irish Injured Jockeys Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Winning hurdler and although 0-8 chasing finished second in this race two years ago and went close on a few occasions last year; going okay when unseating three from home when back chasing at Thurles when last seen in November; may need this but goes well here so worth consideration.

RaceName: Support The Irish Injured Jockeys Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Dual point-to-point winner (soft) is 0-4 in hunter chases and dropped out tamely after forcing much of running on handicap debut at Roscommon last month; first run on ground this quick and hard to have confidence in.

RaceName: Support The Irish Injured Jockeys Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Both hurdle wins came on easier ground but has been running well in defeat on good ground this year; having just third run over fences when third of 4 finishers in 2m5f Cartmel novices' handicap last month, only weakening late on; back in trip here and not one to rule out.

RaceName: Support The Irish Injured Jockeys Handicap Chase
Spotlight: 0-13 under rules for previous trainers, struggling in handicap hurdles when last seen; absent since January and hard to be confident in on chase debut straight into handicap company.

RaceName: Support The Irish Injured Jockeys Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Down Royal handicap hurdle winner last year (2m2f, good) is 0-5 chasing, encouraging Thurles debut effort yet to be built on, unseating at the first over today's C&D last month; may yet improve but opposable for now.

RaceName: Support The Irish Injured Jockeys Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Wexford specialist, winning twice over hurdles last year and off the mark chasing in C&D handicap last month, overcoming attentions of loose horse at the last in outstaying L'Amiral Fromentin by 3.75l (good); up 6lb but major player.

RaceName: Support The Irish Injured Jockeys Handicap Chase
Spotlight: 5yo remains a maiden but generally running well in handicaps, most recently making much of running until outstayed from the last by 3.75l winner Chosen Shant over C&D last month (good); 4lb better off with that rival here so has his chance at revenge.

RaceName: Support The Irish Injured Jockeys Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Second in 2m1f maiden hurdle here last autumn but struggled in handicap hurdles; chase debut at Downpatrick (2m4f) was encouraging but didn't get home as well under a more positive ride here early this month at same trip; down in trip here.

RaceName: Support The Irish Injured Jockeys Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Gained second hurdles win back on his favoured good ground over 2m1f at Clonmel last month but well held off 4lb higher over slightly further 12 days ago; switches to fences now but lack of experience a possible negative.

RaceName: Support The Irish Injured Jockeys Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Won a maiden over hurdles but only placed form (including here) in handicaps; chase debut was a tough one at Tramore in late May, switches to handicap ranks here.

RaceName: Support The Irish Injured Jockeys Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Now 0-4 under rules, chase debut third in Tramore handicap last October probably her best effort; lots to find with a couple of these on last month's C&D debut effort.

RaceName: Support The Irish Injured Jockeys Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Sole win came in 2m5f Bellewstown handicap hurdle last year (good); handicap form in the spring was modest and tries fences again (well held sole previous attempt last summer).

RaceName: Support The Irish Injured Jockeys Handicap Chase
OwnerName: Profit In Your Pocket Racing Club
Spotlight: Veteran bagged a fourth win in 2m1f handicap hurdle here last summer (good); recent hurdles form just okay so more needed on return to fences (hasn't won over fences in nearly 5 years).

RaceName: Dolphin Tec Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Ex-Irish; got off the mark over hurdles at Stratford last July; had three runs on AW in the autumn and makes first handicap start over hurdles in Britain following eight-month layoff; could have a say if fully tuned up.

RaceName: Dolphin Tec Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Scored over hurdles for Olly Murphy last August; below that form for current yard in two runs this term but scored on AW last month; more required back over hurdles.

RaceName: Dolphin Tec Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Got off the mark on his third run over hurdles, following wind surgery, in a maiden over C&D (good) last month, without his usual cheekpieces (also absent today); makes handicap debut and there should be more to come.

RaceName: Dolphin Tec Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Yet to win over hurdles and hasn't kicked on from his promising second at Taunton (2m, good) on his handicap debut in March; first-time cheekpieces need to have a significant impact.

RaceName: Dolphin Tec Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Scored at the third attempt over hurdles in a maiden over C&D (good) last month with first-time tongue-tie (retained); there should be more to come from this mare on her handicap debut.

RaceName: Dolphin Tec Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Two of his five wins over hurdles have been over C&D; has a decent record fresh and made a solid return when runner-up over C&D (good) last month; 5lb higher than his last winning mark though and a career-best is required.

RaceName: Dolphin Tec Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Opened his account over hurdles with a smooth success at Stratford (2m, good to firm) last month; there should be more to come but this looks a better contest so a bit more improvement required.

RaceName: Sky Bet Go-Racing-In-Yorkshire Summer Festival Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 1m2f winner for previous yard and has form figures of 442 over hurdles for current stable; had wind surgery and layoff prior to possibly needing the run when returned to the Flat (33-1) last time; market support would augur well.

RaceName: Sky Bet Go-Racing-In-Yorkshire Summer Festival Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Front-runner who has done all his winning at Newcastle (AW) and Ripon, most recently in May on debut for new stable; has remained in form; should take well to Catterick, given his style of racing; possibilities.

RaceName: Sky Bet Go-Racing-In-Yorkshire Summer Festival Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Gradually coming back to form this term and last two efforts, both at Chester, can be marked up (met late trouble latest); remains on last winning mark and he is 1-1 over C&D (novice success two years ago); respected assuming fortune is back on his side.

RaceName: Sky Bet Go-Racing-In-Yorkshire Summer Festival Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Largely in good form this term, notably two wins in the spring; ran well, beaten narrowly by the market leader, in four-runner field at Yarmouth (11.5f) latest run; upped in grade but receives weight all round; solid chance.

RaceName: Wexford Quay Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Became disappointing over hurdles after winning his maiden and has offered very little in two attempts chasing, including on Tramore comeback in late May; jumping needs improvement.

RaceName: Wexford Quay Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Maiden in all spheres after 47 starts; has run well in defeat at times over fences but hard to see him making the breakthrough here; first-time visor.

RaceName: Wexford Quay Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Down Royal maiden hurdle winner early last year; 0-8 chasing but has shown a useful level of form in beginners' events and when second in 2m1f Cork handicap; dropped out tamely at Punchestown last month over 2m4f so questions to answer on that run.

RaceName: Wexford Quay Beginners Chase
Spotlight: 118-rated hurdler won Bellewstown maiden and Kelso novice late last summer on good ground; two chase efforts underwhelming, may need more time.

RaceName: Wexford Quay Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Dual-purpose 5yo gained his third win on the Flat in 1m5f Navan handicap in April, okay run in better-class Curragh contest following month; hurdles win came in 2m1f maiden here last summer, soft ground was probably against him in subsequent handicaps; back on better ground here could go well if taking to chasing.

RaceName: Wexford Quay Beginners Chase
Spotlight: 119-rated when winning 2m1f Down Royal maiden hurdle at Christmas (yielding); well held in valuable auction series final at Punchestown festival four months later and has had a break since; yard's runners usually expected to improve for the switch fences so greatly respected.

RaceName: Wexford Quay Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Ready winner of 2m Cork maiden hurdle in summer of 2024 (good) but absent since well held in Galway novice a month later; point-to-point winner so inexperience over fences not so much a concern but two-year absence is.

RaceName: Wexford Quay Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Two-time winning hurdler is 0-6 in chases, standout effort when good second in C&D beginners last summer (good); absent since Christmas bit to prove now.

RaceName: Wexford Quay Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Won a point-to-point at four; second in 2m5f maiden hurdle here last year, last month's comeback run in C&D beginners' chase leaves him with a good bit to find but entitled to improve for it.

RaceName: Wexford Quay Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Three wins in 2m4f handicap hurdles, including repeat victory in Clonmel contest in May; none too fluent on chase debut over C&D last month so plenty more needed.

RaceName: Wexford Quay Beginners Chase
Spotlight: Progressive in handicaps in the first half of last season, gaining a third handicap success in 2m4f Clonmel mares' event in October; back to her best in first-time cheekpieces in 2m1f Limerick Grade 3 early this month, staying on for third behind two much higher-rated rivals; dam has already produced a couple of winning chasers so quite possible she could improve for the switch to fences.

RaceName: Bathroom Studio Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Sedgefield winner in September under Freddie Keighley; that form has some substance but he failed to build on it in a higher grade next time; ran well enough on his return at Newton Abbot (2m1f, good) in first-time cheekpieces last month and blinkers are now tried, but he remains on a tough mark.

RaceName: Bathroom Studio Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Still a maiden over hurdles after 13 starts but looked set to put that right at Fontwell (2m3f, good) in April, only to fall at the last when mounting his challenge, after a similar break to today's; up 2lb but Paddy Hanlon retains the ride and this 6yo is worth considering.

RaceName: Bathroom Studio Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 0-8 over hurdles but made the frame on all three starts since switched to handicaps, most recently at Market Rasen (extended 2m5f; just in front of Estacas) in June with a tongue-tie added; looked hard work there and cheekpieces are now tried, but Sean Bowen returns and he's a leading contender.

RaceName: Bathroom Studio Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Dual hurdle winner in late 2024 but pulled up on both starts (over fences/hurdles) since an absence; cheekpieces (worn on last three starts) are removed; others make more obvious appeal.

RaceName: Bathroom Studio Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Couldn't hold on when attempting to make all at Market Rasen (extended 2m5f, good) in June but that was still a solid effort, only narrowly denied second by Little Ledgend late on; should go well again, with Luke Scott able to claim 3lb this time.

RaceName: Bathroom Studio Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Gained a tenth career success when scoring over fences at Fontwell in October off a 13lb higher  mark, but ended the year with two pulled-up efforts; returns from a 277-day break with something to prove back over hurdles, but he's bounced back from quiet spells before.

RaceName: Bathroom Studio Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Encouraging hurdle debut when 12l second at Doncaster (2m, good to soft) in November and ran to a similar level when fifth at Haydock the following month before pulling up in January; has since undergone wind surgery and his pedigree suggests this longer trip will suit on handicap/stable debut for Dan Skelton; interesting contender.

RaceName: Bathroom Studio Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: On a long losing sequence but returned to some form when back from a break and finishing a 12l third of five over C&D 12 days ago, ridden by Rian Corcoran; respected off 4lb lower.

RaceName: Bathroom Studio Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Hasn't beaten a rival on the Flat this year since switching stables and has been well held in three hurdle starts, although he wasn't beaten far when sixth of nine here (2m) in May; moves up in trip for handicap hurdle debut with something to prove.

RaceName: Bathroom Studio Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Back on track to some extent when runner-up at Hereford (2m3f) in first-time cheekpieces, which are retained, albeit beaten 21l; more is needed again.

RaceName: Bathroom Studio Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Twice runner-up in Irish points but has only hinted at ability in three novice/maiden hurdles (2m-2m1f) for this stable; needs to take a marked step forward on this switch to handicaps but the move up in trip should suit.

RaceName: Bathroom Studio Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Built on June's reappearance when keeping on for third over 2m here 12 days ago, in a race she had won in 2024 and 2025; yet to prove she needs this far but is on a good mark and could figure if staying the trip.

RaceName: Bathroom Studio Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 1-39 but was runner-up at Southwell (2m4f, good) in June and ran well for a long way from the front  at Market Rasen last month before fading into fourth; enters calculations on the strength of his earlier effort.

RaceName: Racing Again 4th August Handicap
Spotlight: 4yo maiden who has raced mainly at further; two duck eggs this term leave a doubt over current form; chance of revival depends on the effects of this belated return to sprinting, combined with first-time visor.

RaceName: Racing Again 4th August Handicap
Spotlight: Largely in good form this season, notably for Thirsk win; respectable third at Carlisle most recently; return to a sharper scenario looks ideal, as her broadly consistent record at Catterick attests; solid claims.

RaceName: Racing Again 4th August Handicap
Spotlight: Three-time AW scorer who holds weak claims on her two efforts since joining new yard and returned to turf, last of five at Catterick (5f) latest; 0-8 in this sphere; others preferred.

RaceName: Racing Again 4th August Handicap
Spotlight: Seven-time AW winner who is 1-7 on turf; paid for setting a strong pace when returned to this sphere last time but ended up achieving nothing in terms of form and he has become inconsistent.

RaceName: Racing Again 4th August Handicap
Spotlight: Made all at Thirsk (6f) on penultimate start and was hindered by being pestered for the lead at Beverley latest (eventually dropped away); tactical considerations aside, he remains well treated on peak form and is a course winner.

RaceName: Racing Again 4th August Handicap
Spotlight: Best efforts this term when runner-up at Redcar and Hamilton in May/June; losing run is mounting up but he's capable of taking a serious hand; registered sole success (May 2025) off 8lb higher.

RaceName: Racing Again 4th August Handicap
Spotlight: Has proved an inconsistent maiden but ran encouragingly off a reduced mark at Carlisle last time when dropped back to 6f, returned to turf and back from a layoff; possibilities provided she builds on that effort.

RaceName: WTW Willis Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Hurdle winner has taken well to chasing, winning two of her five starts; won a Killarney beginners' last October and a Limerick handicap on return, both over approx' 3m; fell at Down Royal in between; up 4lb but should go well again.

RaceName: WTW Willis Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Has a poor win record, 1-13 over hurdles and 1-30 over fences; however has been in the frame lots of times including last four chase starts up to 3m2f; should go well again off an unchanged mark.

RaceName: WTW Willis Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Maiden over hurdles opened her account when winning a Down Royal handicap chase in May; was below that level at Roscommon on soft since but can do better back on lively ground.

RaceName: WTW Willis Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Maiden in all spheres but has shown some ability; best chase run was on debut when a 6l third at Down Royal in April; worth watching for market confidence on handicap debut; tongue-tie.

RaceName: WTW Willis Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Got off the mark in a 3m handicap hurdle here in May and was a close second on chasing debut back here over 2m7f; a 4lb rise for that is fair and should be more to come so looks a player.

RaceName: WTW Willis Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Got off the mark over fences when taking this race last year but hasn't repeated that form since between hurdles and fences; however, is 1lb lower than a year ago so has to be respected.

RaceName: WTW Willis Handicap Chase
OwnerName: Desmond O'Brien & John T O'Neill & J P Flavin
Spotlight: Won on chase debut over 2m3f here in 2024; scored twice over hurdles since but was beaten a long way back over fences here last time; has to rebound.

RaceName: WTW Willis Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Dual hurdle winner including once here, is 0-14 over fences but has been placed lots of times including third in this race last year; pulled-up over hurdles last time but a player if rebounding.

RaceName: PJ Nicholls KGM Korea Genuinely Made Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Back over hurdles just four days after a below-par chase run at Cartmel; twice runner-up in this sphere during the summer, including over C&D in refitted cheekpieces last month; major player if bouncing back to that level.

RaceName: PJ Nicholls KGM Korea Genuinely Made Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Nine-race maiden but went close when beaten a head at Stratford on stable debut for Dan Skelton, his first attempt at 2m6f; was well backed and saw the trip out strongly, offering encouragement he'll stay this slightly longer distance; up 4lb but remains a leading contender.

RaceName: PJ Nicholls KGM Korea Genuinely Made Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: A C Bound & Mrs C J Djivanovic
Spotlight: Stratford winner over an extended 3m2f in July 2024 and backed that up with a couple of solid seconds, latterly at Huntingdon (3m1f, good) off a 5lb higher mark that October; has a long absence to overcome but he's been given a chance by the handicapper; market support would be significant.

RaceName: PJ Nicholls KGM Korea Genuinely Made Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Has struggled to recapture his earlier promise since returning from almost a year off in March and has made little impact in two handicap starts this summer, most recently when pulled up over C&D three weeks ago; may be capable of better but first-time blinkers need to prove a positive addition.

RaceName: PJ Nicholls KGM Korea Genuinely Made Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Showed more in a first-time visor when third at Market Rasen after a three-month break last month and backed that up with a 5.25l fifth of 12 on handicap debut there 17 days ago; eased 1lb in the weights and another step forward would put him firmly in the picture.

RaceName: PJ Nicholls KGM Korea Genuinely Made Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Just 1-27 under rules, with his sole success coming more than two years ago; recent efforts have offered some encouragement with the addition of cheekpieces and he's been eased a further 2lb, but still has something to prove with blinkers now tried.

RaceName: PJ Nicholls KGM Korea Genuinely Made Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Three-time hurdle winner, all at around this trip on good ground; he needs things to fall right and remains on a challenging mark, though a reproduction of his Southwell second last month would give him place claims.

RaceName: PJ Nicholls KGM Korea Genuinely Made Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Has yet to show anything worthwhile in five hurdle starts, including in two handicap runs since cheekpieces were fitted; steps up in trip with a tongue-tie added after a 112-day break but has plenty to prove.

RaceName: PJ Nicholls KGM Korea Genuinely Made Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: French claiming hurdle winner on debut in April 2024 and opened his British account under a typically strong Sean Bowen ride over 2m4f here in May; pulled up next time but quickly back on track when runner-up here (2m4f) 12 days ago; Sean Bowen returns and he's a leading contender if seeing out this longer trip.

RaceName: PJ Nicholls KGM Korea Genuinely Made Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Four-time hurdle winner, latest at Kelso (3m, good) in May 2025 off 15lb higher, but well below that level in two runs since returning from a break last month; best watched for now.

RaceName: PJ Nicholls KGM Korea Genuinely Made Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Still a maiden after eight hurdle starts (0-12 overall) but ended a run of poor efforts when a clear second at Ffos Las (3m, good to soft) in May; 4lb out of the weights here and needs to build on that performance.

RaceName: PJ Nicholls KGM Korea Genuinely Made Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won her third point when beating two rivals in April; made an encouraging stable/chase debut when second at Stratford (extended 2m3f, good to firm) last month but may have done too much without the hood when fading into fourth over 2m6f there ten days ago; reverts to hurdles from out of the weights and others make greater appeal.

RaceName: Racing Again Tomorrow 23rd July Family Day Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Got off the mark when winning a Listowel handicap hurdle over just short of 3m last time; point winner has to be respected on chasing debut.

RaceName: Racing Again Tomorrow 23rd July Family Day Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Kilbeggan maiden hurdle winner last year has run some fine races in defeat over fences since; however, has been poor on last three starts and has to rebound.

RaceName: Racing Again Tomorrow 23rd July Family Day Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Won for the second time at this track when scoring in May but was pulled-up at Punchestown since; coming back here is a plus.

RaceName: Racing Again Tomorrow 23rd July Family Day Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Dual hunter chase winner ran a solid race in a Cartmel handicap over a similar trip to this last time; can be involved with a repeat.

RaceName: Racing Again Tomorrow 23rd July Family Day Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Maiden over hurdles; didn't jump that fluently but still ran a decent race at Downpatrick on chasing debut last time; one to consider now in a handicap.

RaceName: Racing Again Tomorrow 23rd July Family Day Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Won a Kilbeggan handicap hurdle in April but soundly beaten over hurdles and fences since so has to do much better.

RaceName: Racing Again Tomorrow 23rd July Family Day Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Won over hurdles at Punchestown and solid efforts twice after in that discipline; however, was beaten 20l over fences at Killarney five days ago.

RaceName: Racing Again Tomorrow 23rd July Family Day Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Showed just a hint of ability for Mouse Morris and fell on return over hurdles; others appeal more on stable debut.

RaceName: Racing Again Tomorrow 23rd July Family Day Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Sole win in 25 chase runs came at this track in 2022; back from a break and others appeal more.

RaceName: Racing Again Tomorrow 23rd July Family Day Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Placed in a point; maiden over hurdles was poor in that sphere last time and others make more appeal on chase debut.

RaceName: Racing Again Tomorrow 23rd July Family Day Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Has been in decent form lately, winning at Downpatrick over 2m7f and third here over 2m3f since; going back up in trip will suit and can go well.

RaceName: Racing Again Tomorrow 23rd July Family Day Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Beaten a long way in three handicap chases lately and doesn't make any appeal.

RaceName: Racing Again Tomorrow 23rd July Family Day Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Maiden has been pulled-up over hurdles and fences the last twice and has to rebound in a major way.

RaceName: Racing Again Tomorrow 23rd July Family Day Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Both chase wins have come on soft ground; in poor form since last autumn and makes no appeal.

RaceName: Racing Again Tomorrow 23rd July Family Day Handicap Chase
OwnerName: Exors of the Late David Cotter
Spotlight: Was a close second over fences at Sligo last October but no sign of that form in different spheres since; still, can't be dismissed for these connections.

RaceName: Matchbook Exchange Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Showed his inexperience first time out at Fairyhouse, started slowly and generally looked raw; top stable is in good form, and he should know more about it now.

RaceName: Matchbook Exchange Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Ardad colt is closely related to stakes-placed US 1m turf 2yo winner Iriseach, half-brother to 1m winner Far From Dandy; dam 7f winner, half-sister to Group-placed winner Sky Seven.

RaceName: Matchbook Exchange Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Gun Runner colt is the first foal of Queen Mary/Prix Morny and Commonwealth Cup winner Campanelle; Jack Cleary aboard, with Wayne Lordan on Man's Best Friend.

RaceName: Matchbook Exchange Irish EBF Maiden
OwnerName: M Tabor & D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & Westerberg
Spotlight: No Nay Never colt was apprentice-ridden and one of two stable outsiders on debut at the Curragh last month; emerged with credit in finishing fourth behind the subsequent Superlative Stakes runner-up Abraham Lincoln, runner-up Haffner also franked the form at a lower level at Newmarket; likely to prosper over further, but should be hard to beat all the same.

RaceName: Matchbook Exchange Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: $125,000 Y; E200,000 2yo; fifth foal; half-brother to Canadian 6.5f/7f AW winner A Little Frisky (inc 2yo Listed); dam Canadian 6f AW 2yo winner, half-sister to useful and prolific 7.5f-1m1f winner Conquest Sandman.

RaceName: Matchbook Exchange Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Always at the back when one of the outsiders on debut at Fairyhouse; blinkers tried now.

RaceName: Matchbook Exchange Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: E82,000 F, E125,000 Y; seventh foal; brother to 1m/1m2f winner Antipodes (RPR 105), half-brother to four winners inc Mackaar (8.6f 2yo/1m2f AW; 101) and Lexington Belle (Listed-placed 7f-8.6f inc 2yo/AW; 94); dam unplaced 7f/10.5f in France (66).

RaceName: Matchbook Exchange Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: 10-1 on debut in a 5f maiden at Cork in May; never showed and finished ninth of ten; safe to rule out.

RaceName: Matchbook Exchange Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Second foal; half-brother to UAE 1m turf winner Samhahn (RPR 90); dam unraced half-sister to winners Franklyn (1m-1m3f) and Waterboy (1m), out of unraced half-sister to French Derby winner Study Of Man, family of top-class miler/broodmare Miesque.

RaceName: Matchbook Exchange Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Dropped away in the final quarter-mile when 100-1 chance first time out at the Curragh.

RaceName: Matchbook Exchange Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: 50-1 when finishing towards the rear in a 17-runner contest at Navan on recent debut.

RaceName: Matchbook Exchange Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Half-brother to a French winner; dam was a Group 3-winning sprinter, closely related to 6.5f Group 1 winner Polydream (Prix Maurice de Gheest), half-sister a to 7f/1m Listed winner.

RaceName: Matchbook Exchange Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Raced a bit too keenly and dropped away to finish in rear in a 5f maiden at this venue.

RaceName: Matchbook Exchange Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Ran in a strong maiden at the Curragh on her second outing, possible nursery type later on.

RaceName: Matchbook Exchange Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Last of 13 in the strong Curragh maiden also contested by Exchange Student.

RaceName: Matchbook Exchange Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: 65,000gns yearling, 200,000gns Breeze Up purchase; slowly away and ran green when odds-on for a Cork maiden won by the smart Ballinea Star; likely to pose a more serious threat now.

RaceName: Matchbook Exchange Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Well held by New Bond Street on Cork running, made little impact at Fairyhouse on second outing.

RaceName: Matchbook Exchange Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Dam won over 7f at two; unlikely to be one of the main players first time out in a race in which several top stables are represented.

RaceName: Forte Healthcare Handicap
Spotlight: Three-time winner has been running well this season but yet to get his head in front; all his victories have come over 6f at Fairyhouse; another solid display at that venue two weeks ago when second in a seven-runner contest; jockey reported interference.

RaceName: Forte Healthcare Handicap
Spotlight: In good form at Dundalk early in the year; third when favourite for a 7f handicap; showed improvement when going up to 1m, recording back-to-back wins; might find this trip inadequate on his first outing after a break.

RaceName: Forte Healthcare Handicap
Spotlight: Navan maiden winner last season; placed over C&D and 5f at Cork before fifth of 12 at the Curragh, again over the minimum trip; needs to find a little extra to open his handicap account; this near-6f trip should suit.

RaceName: Forte Healthcare Handicap
Spotlight: Seven wins, but none yet in Ireland after five attempts, rated 113 at his peak after finishing second in the 2023 Commonwealth Cup; fourth of nine at Down Royal on last, when running off a career-low mark of 75, drop in trip may help after five 7f runs for this yard.

RaceName: Forte Healthcare Handicap
Spotlight: Won a 5f Cork maiden last August (good); out of her depth in a Longchamp Group 3 later that month; fourth in a conditions race at Dundalk in September; should be better for her run at Fairyhouse three weeks ago on first attempt at this trip; signs are that she can stay well enough.

RaceName: Forte Healthcare Handicap
Spotlight: Only one win from 21 starts; runner-up five times; made an encouraging start to the campaign when fourth of 22 at the Curragh in May; subsequent running at the same venue looked too bad to be true

RaceName: Forte Healthcare Handicap
Spotlight: Breeze-up purchase cost £205,000; justified favouritism on debut at Wolverhampton (6f); failed to build in two subsequent outings in Britain, including when highly tried in a Group 3 event at Kempton; runner-up on the second of two Dundalk nursery outings; could be well primed for return.

RaceName: Forte Healthcare Handicap
OwnerName: Mrs S Bury & Ralph Kelly & Odhran O'Neill & Thomas Cox
Spotlight: Six-time runner-up; finally got a winning turn at Navan 11 days ago, wearing blinkers for the first time on his 26th outing; 6lb higher, fair chance if the blinkers do the trick again.

RaceName: Forte Healthcare Handicap
Spotlight: Won over C&D last summer and fair fourth over 5f here three weeks ago; confidence dented by his last two outings at Listowel and Navan.

RaceName: Forte Healthcare Handicap
OwnerName: Team Valor International LLC & A Oliver
Spotlight: Dropped away quickly on handicap/seasonal debut at Fairyhouse in May; close-up Navan fourth last time was reassuring and more in keeping with a 3l second in a maiden at the same venue on his third and final start at two.

RaceName: attheraces.com/marketmovers Handicap
Spotlight: Unraced at two; won five-runner Wolverhampton novice (8.5f, AW) in September and similar form since entering handicaps in October; two turf runs were his worst but was gelded earlier this year and it's still early days on this surface.

RaceName: attheraces.com/marketmovers Handicap
Spotlight: Confirmed earlier juvenile promise when taking 7f AW maiden here (first-time hood) in September; solid effort when third of 12 upped to this trip at Salisbury in May and was unlucky not to finish closer when fourth at Ripon (also good) five weeks ago; hood removed but he still looks a key player.

RaceName: attheraces.com/marketmovers Handicap
Spotlight: Similar run of juvenile form included a win in Newcastle novice (1m, AW) last year and it was a first win since when taking Nottingham handicap (good to firm) over this trip three weeks ago; 3lb nudge but the runner-up has won since; firm contender.

RaceName: attheraces.com/marketmovers Handicap
Spotlight: Unexposed sort who stepped it up a little when third of seven on his handicap debut at Epsom (good to firm) a fortnight ago; that was his first crack at this trip and Oisin Murphy now takes the reins; should be capable of better; interesting.

RaceName: British EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Caught the eye when keeping on for third on her debut at Southwell (6f, AW) last month and built on that when winning at Ffos Las (5f, good; hung left) eight days ago; leading contender under a penalty.

RaceName: British EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Seven-race maiden whose best effort came when a 1l second of 20 in a sales race at Newmarket (6f, good to firm) last August; not yet replicated that form since and left the impression he may be happier back over 6f when third at Nottingham (5f, good to firm; first-time blinkers) 18 days ago.

RaceName: British EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Half-brother to 7f AW winner Gordon Norse (RPR 82); 33-1 but did hint at ability when a never-dangerous fifth of nine on his debut at Nottingham (5f, good to firm; behind Court Alert) 18 days ago; may be one for a slightly longer trip.

RaceName: British EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Down the field in two 6f novice events at Windsor (good to firm) and Yarmouth (soft) last month and looks one for handicaps later on.

RaceName: Arqana Irish EBF Marwell Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Promising introduction when second in a fillies' maiden over C&D; rider reported she was slightly intimidated by the winner two furlongs down; readily justified favouritism at Bellewstown; a lot more on her plate now but worth a shot in this grade, with sustained progress on the cards.

RaceName: Arqana Irish EBF Marwell Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Towards the back on debut at Cork; this is highly ambitious against that background.

RaceName: Arqana Irish EBF Marwell Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
OwnerName: OMEGA Racing & Belle Morris & Russell B Jones Jr
Spotlight: Stepped up from her debut fifth at the Curragh to win a 5f Cork maiden on soft ground; appeared not to stay 6f in a Group 2 at the Curragh, fading into fourth behind the Albany Stakes runner-up; hood used then is discarded for this return to the minimum trip.

RaceName: Arqana Irish EBF Marwell Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Battled well to beat subsequent winner Divine Whisper by a short head at York on third start; down the field in the Queen Mary; met interference when fourth in a C&D Listed race early this month; her once-raced stablemate Concorde Landed looks superior.

RaceName: Arqana Irish EBF Marwell Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Sioux Nation filly; E200,000 breeze up buy in May; made a favourable first impression with a 5l C&D win; fourth-placed Shi Yang won at the Curragh last Saturday; very promising sort, holds entries in the Lowther and the Moyglare.

RaceName: Arqana Irish EBF Marwell Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
Spotlight: 25-1 on debut at Bellewstown when ran on well in the closing stages; faces a huge task here.

RaceName: Arqana Irish EBF Marwell Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Ordinary form in maidens at Listowel and Down Royal; stepped up considerably on those runs to win at Navan on a superb day for her trainer; up against it at this level

RaceName: Arqana Irish EBF Marwell Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Debut Salisbury winner; ran well enough in the Queen Mary to be rated a serious contender; received a bump in the early stages, and had plenty to do before staying on to finish seventh (fifth of 13 in her group) behind the Irish-trained Victorious.

RaceName: Arqana Irish EBF Marwell Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Overcame inexperience to win at Beverley; takes a big jump in class but might not be out of place in this grade; trainer won this with the well-fancied Miss Lamai two years ago.

RaceName: Eden Insurance Brokers Handicap
OwnerName: R Miles, V Miles, T Stamp & Partner
Spotlight: He wasn't disgraced off this much lower turf mark when fifth at Bath (2m1f, good) last month and romped away with Wolverhampton handicap (16.5f, AW) a fortnight later; posted another good effort on AW last time and a first turf win isn't out of the question.

RaceName: Eden Insurance Brokers Handicap
Spotlight: Sole win came in a 2m AW handicap for Dominic Ffrench Davis last June; also acts on turf but he was beaten a long way in a basement event at Epsom (1m4f, good) on his stable debut three weeks ago; goes back up in trip in an added visor.

RaceName: Eden Insurance Brokers Handicap
Spotlight: All six of her wins came on turf, the last three over 1m6f; she hasn't won since September 2023, however, and was beaten 22l over C&D (good) on her reappearance in May; will need to have derived plenty of benefit from that.

RaceName: Eden Insurance Brokers Handicap
Spotlight: Kept on when fifth of nine on his handicap debut over 1m2f at Nottingham (good) in April and he then won the first juvenile hurdle of the jump season in June; may have been unlucky not to make it two when beaten by Lucky Sevens at Stratford ten days ago and he has clear potential now asked to tackle a staying trip in this sphere.

RaceName: Eden Insurance Brokers Handicap
Spotlight: Three-time winner at up to 1m2f on AW for Ian Williams but he's yet to click in four starts around 1m4f for this yard; new trip needs to spark a revival.

RaceName: Eden Insurance Brokers Handicap
Spotlight: Unraced at two and it was just his second handicap when off the mark at Yarmouth (1m, good to firm; first-time cheekpieces) in April; didn't appear to stay 11.5f here in May but he won again at Brighton (1m2f) the following month and got the better of Tiny Riot over hurdles ten days ago; interesting his shrewd yard is happy for him to tackle 2m in this sphere.

RaceName: Eden Insurance Brokers Handicap
Spotlight: Her fifth of 13 in Windsor classified (11.5f, good to soft; first-time cheekpieces) was a first hint of ability six weeks ago but that hasn't been built on in two starts over 1m4f this month; does have an Ascot Gold Cup winner in her pedigree so might not be a forlorn hope upped to 2m.

RaceName: Every Race Live On Racing TV Handicap
Spotlight: Well held on her debut at Kempton (7f, AW) in December and has run to a similar level in two starts on turf this year; steps up to 1m with a hood added for her handicap debut and she may well do better.

RaceName: Every Race Live On Racing TV Handicap
Spotlight: Doubled her tally when making all in a soft-ground classified event at Ayr (7f) last month before finishing a good second in a handicap there (good) next time; below her best over 7f here last week and this free-going sort will need to settle now back at 1m.

RaceName: Every Race Live On Racing TV Handicap
Spotlight: Has shown improved form since returning from a two-month break, winning at Windsor (1m, good to firm) in May and three good efforts since, most recently when a 1l second of seven at Chepstow (1m, good to firm); once again has strong claims.

RaceName: Every Race Live On Racing TV Handicap
Spotlight: Big prices and beaten at least 8.5l in each of her three starts but she did show some promise when a never-dangerous sixth of 12 at Lingfield (1m, AW; first-time hood) two weeks ago; hood is retained and she could yet do better now tackling a handicap.

RaceName: Every Race Live On Racing TV Handicap
Spotlight: Eight-race maiden who has been well beaten in both starts on turf, including when beating only one rival home in the race won by Buckland Belle at Windsor (1m, good to firm) in May; others preferred.

RaceName: Every Race Live On Racing TV Handicap
Spotlight: Nine-race maiden who was runner-up on Kempton AW last August but she's been well beaten in six starts on turf, including at Yarmouth (1m, good to firm) three weeks ago; first-time cheekpieces need to make a difference.

RaceName: Yeomanstown Stud Irish EBF Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: Leopardstown Listed winner at two (7f) hasn't won again since but ran well in Group 3 there last summer over same trip; just okay runs both starts this term, blinkers tried at same track last time; takes a drop in trip here which could prove troublesome.

RaceName: Yeomanstown Stud Irish EBF Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: Very promising start to her career, winning a Curragh 2yo maiden in September 2024 before narrowly denied in a rated race over today's C&D on last summer's comeback run; some okay runs this season, not least on latest when good fourth (having had to be switched) in Curragh Listed last month; one to consider.

RaceName: Yeomanstown Stud Irish EBF Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies & Mares)
OwnerName: Mrs Michael J Byrne & Jerry P Murphy
Spotlight: Sole win in 19 starts came in Cork maiden but has strung together a series of good efforts in decent sprint company this season, notably seconds in Listed events both here and at Cork, most recently third in conditions event here early in the month; best form at 5f so possibly vulnerable at this longer trip.

RaceName: Yeomanstown Stud Irish EBF Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: Likeable mare ran a cracking race on her return from the AW to finish a close third in Fairyhouse conditions event (6f, good) in June; not at same level later in month in Curragh Listed and good bit to find with Fregada on that run.

RaceName: Yeomanstown Stud Irish EBF Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies & Mares)
OwnerName: Mary B Hogan & Michael M O'Brien
Spotlight: Progressing really well at 5f this campaign, recording back-to-back handicap wins last month at Cork and Down Royal and only narrowly denied the hat-trick at the Curragh in the valuable Rockinghan; not sure the return to 6f will suit but thriving and well worth this shot at Listed level.

RaceName: Yeomanstown Stud Irish EBF Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: Won a C&D handicap off 75 last season; safely held since, most recently seasonal return in Curragh Listed last month; lots to find.

RaceName: Yeomanstown Stud Irish EBF Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: Series of placings in maidens and has made an immediate impact in handicaps, winning at Down Royal (7f, soft) and early this month back at 6f when getting up late at Fairyhouse on quicker ground; clearly progressive but this represents a big jump in class.

RaceName: Yeomanstown Stud Irish EBF Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: Starman filly who took her record to 3-4 when seeing off eight rivals in 6f Goodwood Listed race (good) in early May and better again when third in Group 3 Summer Stakes at York 12 days ago, having shown plenty of pace; sets a good standard and major chance.

RaceName: Yeomanstown Stud Irish EBF Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: Wolverhampton maiden winner has been tried at Listed level in all three starts since, notably at Ayr last month when career-best second over 5f in Land O'Burns Stakes; back to 6f here and this stiff finish may not suit.

RaceName: Yeomanstown Stud Irish EBF Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: Mixed form last season but has come good on latest two starts, in AW maiden in February and 11 days ago in Limerick rated race (6.5f) staying on late to assert; type to continue improving but this a considerable step up in class.

RaceName: Yeomanstown Stud Irish EBF Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: Landed Bellewstown maiden from the front early this month (5f, good) and ran well for a long way in Curragh handicap on Sunday; different kettle of fish here though.

RaceName: Yeomanstown Stud Irish EBF Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: In good form this season since joining this yard, winning twice and career-best second in Group 3 Lacken Stakes over C&D in May (good); well below that level however both starts since at Listed level (hung left latest); visor now replaces hood.

RaceName: Yeomanstown Stud Irish EBF Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: Fairyhouse nursery winner last season her sole win but huge improvement to finish third in Group 3 Lacken Stakes over C&D in May; not at same level in Curragh Listed on latest so more needed.

RaceName: Yeomanstown Stud Irish EBF Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: Landed Cork maiden last month and improved hugely early this month when only reeled in close home by a 102-rated rival (Likedbymike third) in five-runner conditions event here (5f, good); has lots of speed, not sure this extra furlong will play to her strengths.

RaceName: Yeomanstown Stud Irish EBF Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: Lightly raced filly whose four runs have all been over further, notably Dundalk AW maiden win at two; last month's comeback run in Navan Listed was disappointing (slow to break) but remains open to improvement; drops in trip with cheekpieces tried.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Form in four starts has a slightly uneven look to it and she finished sixth of eight on her nursery debut at Yarmouth (5f, good to firm) a fortnight ago; she'll need more in the retained cheekpieces

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Beaten favourite in a 5f novice at Southwell (AW) on his debut in May; gelded and blinkered for his second run when showing some improvement in Chester maiden (6f, good) last month but he didn't build on that in Leicester seller, when also looking quirky, 18 days ago; still early days, however, and the drop back to 5f looks a good move for his first nursery.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Tail swisher who ran well when second of seven in Chepstow nursery (6f, good to firm) a fortnight ago; makes first start at 5f since her debut and she's respected with Billy Loughnane taking over.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: She had Dee's Star and debutante Romidijo behind when encouraging fourth of 12 at Ascot (5f, good) in May on her second start; hasn't kicked on in a maiden and novice subsequently but maybe Oisin Murphy can help her get back on track in this nursery debut.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Failed to improve across four qualifying runs but her fifth of 11 in Newbury novice (6f, good to firm) wasn't a bad effort a fortnight ago; didn't shape like she was begging to be tried at 5f that day, however; nursery debut.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Second start and she was one place behind Miss Moneypit when fifth of 12 in Ascot novice (5f, good) in May; that was encouraging so finishing last in a Musselburgh seller one month later takes some explaining; blinkers go on for this first nursery and it's too soon to write her off.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Finished just over 2l behind Romidijo when fifth on her nursery debut at Chepstow (6f, good to firm) a fortnight ago; raced very freely that day so this return to 5f should help and there's a suspicion we've yet to see the best of her.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Today Fillies' Handicap
OwnerName: Lady Bamford , Mrs Collins & Bv Sangster
Spotlight: Made a winning handicap debut at Ffos Las (1m2f, soft) last month before going close at Nottingham (1m2f, good to firm), then beat three rivals at Ffos Las (1m4f) eight days ago; should have more to offer and she's respected under a 6lb penalty (goes up same amount in future).

RaceName: Join Racing TV Today Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Ran well when second of seven on handicap debut at Goodwood (1m2f, soft) last month but she was said to have moved poorly when tailed off there one week later; that needs forgiving but she may bounce back.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Today Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Off the mark at the sixth attempt when winning at Thirsk (1m4f, good) in May off 5lb lower but she seemed stretched by 1m6f on soft at Nottingham since; this drop back in trip looks a good move and she has to be respected.

RaceName: O'Neill & Co. Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Four AW wins; 1-20 on turf; failed to make much impact in first two starts for this stable; despite not much luck in running, better when seventh of 15 at Limerick; fifth at Leopardstown a month ago suggests he could be competitive off a career-low mark.

RaceName: O'Neill & Co. Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Two Flat wins came in 2024; last month's third over 1m1f at Leopardstown was encouraging; unplaced twice this month; closely matched with Medieval Night on last week's Killarney run.

RaceName: O'Neill & Co. Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Maiden's best efforts have come in Leopardstown handicaps, including when a staying-on fourth of 22 (having hung left) on penultimate start; failed to match that form at Killarney last time.

RaceName: O'Neill & Co. Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: A 22-race maiden, placed ten times, including five in a good spell between July and October last season; reported coughing at Down Royal on seasonal/stable debut; better in mid-field at Leopardstown.

RaceName: O'Neill & Co. Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: A 17-race maiden; plenty to prove at this stage; failed to run to expectations at Killarney last week; quite well fancied then, at a venue where he has produced some of his better efforts; blinkered now.

RaceName: O'Neill & Co. Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Two wins to his name; best effort in four runs this year when close third at Limerick (7f, yielding) in May; slow break costly at that track a day later; not beaten far in fifth at Bellewstown early this month; first time with cheekpieces.

RaceName: O'Neill & Co. Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Was in the form of his life around this time two seasons ago with back-to-back 6f wins for Michael O'Callaghan; signs of a revival when seventh of 21 at Naas last month; another reasonably encouraging run when fourth at Leopardstown last week.

RaceName: O'Neill & Co. Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Sole win came on the beach at Laytown last September; yet to make his presence felt this term.

RaceName: O'Neill & Co. Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: 1-42 on turf; struggled on the AW last winter but showed more when fifth at Roscommon in May; failed to repeat that effort at Gowran; one of her better turf efforts at Leopardstown last time; four years since she recorded a 1m victory at that track.

RaceName: O'Neill & Co. Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Went close at Dundalk last August; fair seasonal debut over 1m1f at Leopardstown, was disputing the lead a furlong down before fading; has a bit to find with Famous Enough on recent Dundalk running.

RaceName: O'Neill & Co. Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: A 1-22 turf record is worrying; however, he holds a good chance if reproducing the form of recent Dundalk second, which gives him an edge over Akashaa (sixth).

RaceName: O'Neill & Co. Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Struggled in three handicap attempts at this trip, marginally better over 7f last time; hard to fancy despite reduced mark.

RaceName: O'Neill & Co. Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: No sign of worthwhile form in five maiden outings; fifth of 13 over 7f at Limerick on handicap debut was a step in the right direction; suffered slight interference inside the last furlong; not as good over a slightly shorter trip at the same venue last time.

RaceName: O'Neill & Co. Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Poor form as a juvenile, ran only twice last year; no encouragement from reappearance, 66-1 when in rear in 22-runner Leopardstown contest.

RaceName: O'Neill & Co. Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: All six races in 2025 were over longer distances; hard to fancy unless the change of emphasis makes a big difference.

RaceName: O'Neill & Co. Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Best form at Cork, the scene of her only win (over 7f in September 2024) and last season's best effort, in a 6f contest; failed to make much impact on seasonal debut at Killarney six weeks ago.

RaceName: O'Neill & Co. Apprentice Handicap
OwnerName: Patrick M O'Brien/Larry Murray/Richard W Walsh/Derek Cummins
Spotlight: Is 1-34 on turf; won on heavy over 7f at this track in 2024; reasonably consistent form this season; fifth at Leopardstown 13 days ago matches him closely with Medieval Night and Morning Approach.

RaceName: O'Neill & Co. Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Losing run on turf stretches back to 2022; stronger record on AW but good effort at Bellewstown on latest.

RaceName: Free Race Replays On attheraces.com Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
OwnerName: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid
Spotlight: Made a winning 2yo debut at Newmarket (6f, good) but he missed last year and was gelded in the autumn; shade unlucky not to retain his unbeaten record when beaten a nose at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) on his comeback a fortnight ago; this is a fairly quick second run back from a big absence but he's scared most of them off and is impossible to oppose.

RaceName: Free Race Replays On attheraces.com Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Sent off 66-1 when 21l adrift and failing to beat a rival at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) last Tuesday; guaranteed some prize money this time on his turf debut.

RaceName: Free Race Replays On attheraces.com Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: £50,000 yearling who has the pedigree to win races but his two starts have been inconclusive due to issues at the start; blinkers go on and he's the most likely to take advantage if Asuka blazes his penalty kick over the bar.

RaceName: Family Sunday 10th August Book Now Handicap
Spotlight: Both wins over 7f on Newcastle AW on his first two starts; has been running well since the visor was added, finishing runner-up at Redcar (1m2f, good to firm) in May and twice finishing fourth at this track (1m4f/1m2f); solid claims.

RaceName: Family Sunday 10th August Book Now Handicap
Spotlight: Sole win from 14 starts came when making all over 1m4f here last July (good) and he was returning from eight months off when a solid third of eight at Epsom (1m4f, good) three weeks ago; won't mind the drop back in trip and has to be considered.

RaceName: Family Sunday 10th August Book Now Handicap
OwnerName: Paul K Spencer And Mrs R Carr
Spotlight: Sole win from 12 starts came in a 1m Lingfield maiden last August when trained by the Crisfords; has dropped a fair way in the weights since joining this yard and he ran well when third over this C&D 18 days ago on first try over this far; can go well again with cheekpieces now added.

RaceName: Family Sunday 10th August Book Now Handicap
Spotlight: Both wins over 1m at Ffos Las (good), the most recent last August off a 4lb lower mark; he ran well when third there four weeks ago but has stamina to prove now stepping back up in trip in first-time cheekpieces.

RaceName: Family Sunday 10th August Book Now Handicap
Spotlight: Doubled his tally when winning here over 1m4f in May, coming from last to first, and he left the impression he's still in form when sixth of 11 at Pontefract two weeks ago; will need a good gallop now dropped back in trip, which he isn't sure to get.

RaceName: Family Sunday 10th August Book Now Handicap
Spotlight: Well held in four starts for James Tate, including on handicap debut at Beverley (1m2f, good to firm; first-time blinkers) four weeks ago; since purchased for 3,400gns; headgear discarded for his stable debut.

RaceName: NFP Handicap
Spotlight: Both turf wins came on quick ground at 7f-1m at Ayr; AW winner last winter and posted career-best on latest when only caught late on in 17-runner Curragh handicap over this trip last month; trip/ground remains in his favour, can go well again.

RaceName: NFP Handicap
Spotlight: Three-time AW winner who sole turf success from nine starts came over this trip at Leopardstown in 2023 off 65; two runs in premier handicaps this season leave him with a bit to find although was out of the handicap last time; down in grade here and runs off his correct mark so don't rule out.

RaceName: NFP Handicap
Spotlight: Off the mark at the fourth attempt in maidens last September at the Curragh (6f); safely held at same venue on last month's return (handicap debut) and plenty to find with second-placed Little Empire on that.

RaceName: NFP Handicap
