Spotlight: AW promise last year and off the mark on second turf run this year, sent on some way out under a positive ride over this trip at Leopardstown 13 days ago, relishing the quicker ground; 8lb rise looks plenty but quite likely has more to offer under these conditions.

RaceName: NFP Handicap
Spotlight: Five wins between turf and AW for Johnny Murtagh; best effort for this yard came when fourth over 7f at the Curragh in April (soft), well held since; different headgear combination now tried.

RaceName: NFP Handicap
Spotlight: Two-time AW winner is 0-14 on turf but has gone close in defeat, including at Down Royal on penultimate; too keen over 1m last time, will appreciate return to 7f.

RaceName: NFP Handicap
Spotlight: Curragh maiden win came on soft but handled quicker ground well in Fairyhouse handicap early this month, albeit changed legs late on when headed; first attempt at 7f this season, possible vulnerable.

RaceName: NFP Handicap
Spotlight: Needed every yard of this trip to justify strong market support on handicap debut at Fairyhouse a fortnight ago, getting up in three-way photo, still looking green; 7lb rise enough but this stiffer 7f should bring more out of him, major player.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: C&D winner last August who subsequently added two AW wins, latterly in five-runner event at Newcastle (7f) off 3lb lower in March; looked a little unlucky when close third over C&D (good) off this mark in May and she's got a solid chance of giving Bahrain Champion Jockey Ebrahim Nader his first winner in Britain.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Tends to win in her turn and it was a sixth success (second on turf) when taking 7f Doncaster handicap (good) five weeks ago; lesser form in two subsequent runs over that C&D, including when borderline tailed off a fortnight ago.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Won two of her first three starts as a juvenile but she went off the boil in the autumn and has been disappointing this year, including in first-time cheekpieces at Pontefract (1m, good) a fortnight ago; declining mark and a visor is given a whirl but only market support would make her of interest.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Fillies' Handicap
OwnerName: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid
Spotlight: Yet to score but after an inauspicious handicap debut/reappearance over 1m2f last month she was back in form with fairly close third of six at Yarmouth (1m, good to firm) four weeks ago; should be okay back at 7f and can't be ruled out.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Reappeared with a winning handicap debut at Doncaster (good to firm) in April but she was miles below that when favourite but tailed off at Salisbury (again 7f, good) four weeks later; that was too bad to be true and subsequent two-month break suggests something possibly came to light; might be worth another chance.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Promising start for this yard when placed in course maidens (7f/7.5f; including cheekpieces) in May and June so her handicap debut was underwhelming when seventh of 11 at Leicester 18 days ago; that came over 6f, however, and the return to this track and trip can be viewed positively; gets a tongue-tie and cheekpieces this time.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Gradually improved her RPRs across three qualifying runs, including when sixth at Kempton (7f, AW) three weeks ago; bred to be a good bit better than this opening mark and she's likely capable of better again; has to be of interest.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Five-time AW winner (including here) but she's 0-13 on turf and was never competitive at Epsom (7f, good to firm) a fortnight ago on her return from a break; might step forward from that but others have more pressing claims.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: 0-3 as a 2yo but she's shown improved form this year, winning a Ripon novice by 7l in May (6f, good; first-time cheekpieces) before following up in a three-runner handicap at Salisbury (6f, good to firm) 11 days ago; now 6lb higher in a more competitive race but she's well related and could progress further.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Won twice as a 2yo and has finished runner-up in three of her four starts this year, most recently at Carlisle (6f, good to firm) two weeks ago; no obvious reason why she won't give her running.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Fillies' Handicap
OwnerName: E S-O Racing Club And Elaine Chivers
Spotlight: Runner-up on her first two outings at Windsor and Newbury (both 6f on good to firm), and got off the mark when making all at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) last month; drops back in trip for her handicap debut and remains open to further progress.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Kept on well for winning debut at Chelmsford (7f, AW) in November; raced too freely over 1m at Goodwood (good to soft) last month but ran a solid race when third of five at Newmarket (7f, good to firm) four weeks ago; now drops in trip for handicap debut (half-sister to a 5f/6f winner).

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Made all at Yarmouth (6f, good) in October off today's mark but she's been well held in three starts since, most recently at Newmarket last month; others have stronger claims.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: 4yo who gained her third success when winning over this C&D on good to firm 18 days ago, beating ten rivals; steps up in grade off a 5lb higher mark but there could be more to come on turf.

RaceName: Naas Racecourse Race
Spotlight: Better than ever last summer, winning Cork premier handicap (7f, good) and career-best second in Scurry Handicap at the Curragh; matched that effort when beaten just a neck by Headmaster in the Habitat Handicap back at the Curragh on seasonal return in May and following month fared best of those racing far side when third in repeat Cork bid; in fine form but stiff enough task conceding weight to a pair of less-exposed 3yo fillies.

RaceName: Naas Racecourse Race
Spotlight: All four runs have been at this trip, notably Leopardstown 2yo maiden win and recent seasonal return when second in 6-runner Group 3 Brownstown Stakes at same track, staying on well after not getting clear run on far rail; remains capable of better and cheekpieces now added.

RaceName: Naas Racecourse Race
Spotlight: Looked potentially very smart last year, winning Group 3 at the Curragh and finishing runner-up in the Group 2 Debutante; two runs this term well below that level however, too free in Group 3 at Leopardstown and never landed a blow over today's C&D when fourth of 6 in rated race in first-time tongue-tie; still respected and yard in fine form

RaceName: Grand Prix de Vichy (Group 3) (Turf)
Spotlight: Comes into this in peak form having won a 1m2f Group 3 last time in San Siro on good ground; track no problem as he was third in this race 12 months ago (also sixth in 2024), but was a place behind Casapueblo last year, and again has to give 2lb to that rival (also behind the Godolphin gelding in March on the AW when giving even more weight away); always runs his race, though, and should be involved.

RaceName: Grand Prix de Vichy (Group 3) (Turf)
Spotlight: Well bred and unexposed 4yo, who has gone on a winning run, successful on his last four starts, which includes two Listed events (one turf one AW) over half a furlong shorter; bit surprising he's been off for a couple of months but has won at this trip on soft ground and should be a player on first start in a Group race; stable won this race last year.

RaceName: Grand Prix de Vichy (Group 3) (Turf)
Spotlight: Reliable performer enjoying a good season with a tongue-tie added, winning his first two starts before narrowly beaten in a Longchamp Group 3 at this trip a month and a half ago; finished runner-up, ahead of Woodchuck, in the 2025 running of this when getting 2lb from that rival, so holds strong claims; Pierre-Charles Boudot rides for first time.

RaceName: Grand Prix de Vichy (Group 3) (Turf)
Spotlight: Often runs in good company but recorded an RPR of 73 two starts ago and 85 last time which, along with her overall profile (not passed the post in the first four since last August), suggests she has plenty on to be placed let alone win.

RaceName: Grand Prix de Vichy (Group 3) (Turf)
Spotlight: Finds it hard to win but nothing wrong with her latest third to the classy Aventure in a Longchamp Group 2 in May (1m2.5f, good to soft); need to back that up, though, and add more to it in order to record a first win since August 2023.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Classified Stakes
Spotlight: 5f turf winner here who scored for the third time when taking 7f Brighton classified event (good) under Donagh Murphy last month; not so good on his next two starts but he's got a good draw and can't be ruled out.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Won similar event here on AW in 2024; also took 6f course handicap last June and was well on top in Chelmsford handicap (7f, AW) in October; was struggling when last seen in the spring, however, and a two-month break will need to have recharged her batteries.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Four wins for Harry Eustace include 7f Brighton handicap (good) around one year ago; did better on her second go for this yard when fourth over that C&D one month ago and holds claims on her first classified attempt for four years.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Nailed a rapid AW classified hat-trick in January for Ian Williams but her subsequent form in handicaps for that yard and this doesn't compare; maybe her return to this sphere will help but she needs to get back on track.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Very moderate form this year and others have much stronger claims.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Placed five times for Sean Woods but he's yet to replicate that form in seven starts for this yard and his return from a break was a particularly modest effort three weeks ago.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Classified Stakes
Spotlight: 7f AW handicap winner in October who went close in a course classified (7.5f, good to firm) 25 days ago; a repeat of that effort puts her firmly in the picture.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Won a handicap (7f) for Michael Attwater last August and it was a solid start for this yard when neck second in 1m classified (again here on AW) last Monday; yet to produce his best on quick turf but Billy Loughnane retains the ride; respected.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Classified Stakes
Spotlight: 50-1 when beaten 17l in AW classified (8.5f) on her stable debut a fortnight ago; still fairly early days but she'll need to show something she's yet to reveal.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Her fourth of five at Salisbury (7f, good) wasn't a bad effort last month but that's the firm pick of her last four starts; Oisin Murphy is an eye-catching booking for this classified debut, however; interesting to see how she goes in the market.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Some solid efforts earlier in the year include a win at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) in February; not so good since April and he was tailed off in six-runner Brighton handicap on his turf debut three weeks ago; now makes classified debut and he'll need to put that firmly behind him; missed the break and never figured when beaten 18l at Wolverhampton yesterday.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Sent off 66-1 when beating one home in similar event here (7.5f) 25 days ago; this is just his fifth start but he'll need to start putting it together.

RaceName: Kube - Leicester's Premier Exhibition Venue Handicap
Spotlight: Landed a Lingfield maiden (6f, AW) in September but has struggled in three handicap starts since, most recently on her reappearance at Kempton in April; off since and now returns off a 3lb lower mark.

RaceName: Kube - Leicester's Premier Exhibition Venue Handicap
Spotlight: Won 7f novice on Southwell AW in September but disappointing in three starts since, including at Newmarket (1m, good; first start since wind surgery) in June; drops back to 6f with a bit to prove; cheekpieces on.

RaceName: Kube - Leicester's Premier Exhibition Venue Handicap
Spotlight: Front-runner who gained his third win when holding on to land a Catterick handicap over 6f in May; ran well at Doncaster (6f, good) next time and it looks worth forgiving last month's Newcastle effort (5f, AW), having raced alone towards the far rail; could go well.

RaceName: Kube - Leicester's Premier Exhibition Venue Handicap
Spotlight: Runner-up on his debut at Thirsk (5f, good) last May but hasn't built on that in four starts since; never featured after being slowly into stride in first-time blinkers at Wolverhampton last month and has since been dropped another 3lb.

RaceName: Kube - Leicester's Premier Exhibition Venue Handicap
Spotlight: Off the mark when making all at Redcar (7f, good) in May and bounced back from a lesser effort when a solid third at Doncaster (6f, good to firm) last month; he isn't the most consistent and cheekpieces now go on.

RaceName: Kube - Leicester's Premier Exhibition Venue Handicap
Spotlight: Modest form when fifth of six at Brighton (6f, good to firm; first-time hood/cheekpieces) and he was reported to have hung left when failing to beat a rival at Kempton (7f, AW) three weeks ago; headgear returns for his handicap debut.

RaceName: Kube - Leicester's Premier Exhibition Venue Handicap
Spotlight: Sole win from nine starts came in a nursery at Brighton (7f, good to firm; first-time cheekpieces) last September; clear second behind an easy winner at Chepstow (6f, good to firm) last month and it was another solid effort at Brighton (7f) three weeks ago; drops back in trip with claims.

RaceName: Kube - Leicester's Premier Exhibition Venue Handicap
Spotlight: 0-8 but he was a front-running second of 11 at Bath (5.5f, good; beaten 3l) last month; however, he dropped away after showing early speed at Windsor (5f, good to firm; reported to have stopped quickly) three weeks ago and needs to bounce back.
